title: Authentication, Authorization, RBAC, Service Accounts, Tokens, and Impersonation · API Object Lens: K8s Authn Authz Admission Chain
summary: A CI workload uses the default service account and a long-lived token while a broad ClusterRoleBinding permits secret reads and workload creation across tenants. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s authn authz admission chain increment in least-privilege identity and authorization policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace TLS identity, authentication, user and group information, authorization, admission, validation, persistence, and audit for one request.
Test load, Pod loss, node drain, zone loss, slow dependency, scaling lag, quota, and recovery while measuring user-visible service objectives.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example workload uses the default ServiceAccount, automounts a broad token, and receives wildcard cluster-admin through a group binding. Derive subject, verb, resource, namespace, token audience, impersonation, and least-privilege proof. Competency probe: Trace TLS identity, authentication, user and group information, authorization, admission, validation, persistence, and audit for one request. A defensible solution applies K8s Authn Authz Admission Chain, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to least-privilege identity and authorization policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-identity-rbac-serviceaccounts-3
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-identity-rbac-serviceaccounts-3
    artifact: rbac-policy.yaml
    decision: worked k8s identity rbac serviceaccounts 3
    environment: shared multi-tenant cluster under Restricted Pod Security
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      serviceAccount: intake/status-api
      automountServiceAccountToken: false
      token: projected-audience-intake-api
      role: get-configmap-status-only
      binding: namespace-intake
      review: auth-can-i-and-escalation-map
    verify: kubectl auth can-i --list --as system:serviceaccount:intake:status-api -n intake
    failure: Reject broad ClusterRoleBinding, long-lived static tokens, wildcard verbs, secret reads, bind or escalate paths, or workload creation as harmless.
    repair: Separate human and workload identity, scope verbs and resources, use bound audience tokens, test authorization, and review escalation periodically.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-authn-authz-admission-chain-go9dk9
  - competency: k8s-authn-authz-admission-chain
    artifact: rbac-policy.yaml
    decision: authn authz admission chain
    environment: three-node dual-stack staging cluster across two zones
    fault-injection: withdraw the selected image digest after one node pulls it
    retained-proof: object UID generation observedGeneration and status conditions
    case-key: go9dk9
    change: |
      serviceAccount: intake/status-api
      automountServiceAccountToken: false
      token: projected-audience-intake-api
      role: get-configmap-status-only
      binding: namespace-intake
      review: auth-can-i-and-escalation-map
    verify: kubectl auth can-i --list --as system:serviceaccount:intake:status-api -n intake
    failure: Reject broad ClusterRoleBinding, long-lived static tokens, wildcard verbs, secret reads, bind or escalate paths, or workload creation as harmless.
    repair: Separate human and workload identity, scope verbs and resources, use bound audience tokens, test authorization, and review escalation periodically.
